events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the assessment directly to the required JSON file. The key issue is impulse-line orientation for the fluid service, which can trap condensate/gas and bias the DP transmitter while it is already in service.

events.label: shell (exit 127)
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && printf '%s
' '{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"high\",
  \"work_stage\": \"in-service\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\", \"degraded\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 100,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.93,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"DP impulse lines are sloped opposite of the required service orientation\",
    \"Gas taps below the pipe can collect liquid in the impulse legs and bias the DP signal\",
    \"Liquid taps on top can trap gas or vapor in the impulse legs and bias the DP signal\",
    \"Installed condition conflicts with accepted transmitter installation practice and manufacturer installation guidance\",
    \"Asset is already in service, so erroneous process indication or control action is credible\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Fail the installation and remove the loop from relied-upon service until corrected or independently verified safe\",
    \"Re-pipe impulse lines with proper tap orientation and continuous slope for the process fluid service\",
    \"Drain or vent impulse legs as appropriate for the service before recommissioning\",
    \"Perform leak check, zero check, calibration/trim verification, and loop check after rework\",
    \"Review affected quality gate records and inspect similar DP transmitter installations for repeated workmanship error\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"The most likely failure mode is a DP transmitter installation error causing trapped liquid in gas service or trapped gas/vapor in liquid service. Wrong tap location and impulse-line slope create unequal or unstable static heads at the transmitter, producing biased, sluggish, or unreliable differential pressure readings. Because the defect is present on an in-service operating asset, it is a non-compliant installed-defective condition with degraded measurement integrity and high process/quality risk.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"API RP 551 - process measurement instrumentation installation practices for impulse piping, tap orientation, slope, venting, and draining by service\",
    \"Manufacturer transmitter installation manuals - DP transmitter impulse-line routing, slope, vent/drain, and commissioning requirements\"
  ]
}' > /app/answer.json
jq . /app/answer.json"
